    58→    And Marcus clicks "Dismiss"
    59→    Then the learning status changes to "dismissed"
    60→    And a success toast shows "Learning dismissed"
    61→    And the card is removed from the pending list
    62→    And the reason is stored with the learning record
    63→
    64→  Scenario: Dismiss requires a reason
    65→    Given Marcus clicks "Dismiss" on a pending learning
    66→    When the dismiss dialog opens
    67→    Then the reason field is required
    68→    And the "Dismiss" button is disabled until a reason is entered
    69→
    70→  Scenario: Cancel approval returns to pending list
    71→    Given Marcus opens the approve dialog for a pending learning
    72→    When Marcus clicks "Cancel"
    73→    Then the dialog closes
    74→    And the learning remains in pending status
    75→    And no changes are made
    76→
    77→  Scenario: Undo approval via toast
    78→    Given Marcus just approved a learning
    79→    And the success toast is visible with an "Undo" action
    80→    When Marcus clicks "Undo" within the toast timeout
    81→    Then the learning reverts to "pending_approval" status
    82→    And the pending count re-increments
    83→

file.content:
Feature: Review & Approve/Dismiss Pending Learning
  As a workspace owner receiving agent learning suggestions,
  I want to review and approve or dismiss each suggestion efficiently,
  so I can maintain governance without bottlenecking the system.

  # Traces to: Job 4 (Approval & Triage)

  Background:
    Given Marcus is logged into workspace "brain-v1"
    And the workspace has the following pending learnings:
      | text                                              | type        | suggested_by    | confidence | agents          |
      | Avoid module-level mutable singletons for caching | instruction | observer_agent  | 0.87       | mcp             |
      | Use RecordId objects instead of string IDs         | constraint  | pm_agent        | 0.92       | mcp, chat_agent |
      | Log all errors with structured context            | instruction | observer_agent  | 0.78       | All agents      |

  Scenario: View pending learnings queue
    Given Marcus navigates to the learning library
    When Marcus clicks the "Pending (3)" tab
    Then 3 pending learning cards are displayed
    And each card shows the learning text
    And each card shows the suggesting agent name
    And each card shows the confidence score as a percentage
    And each card has "Approve" and "Dismiss" buttons

  Scenario: Approve a pending learning without edits
    Given Marcus is on the Pending tab
    When Marcus clicks "Approve" on "Avoid module-level mutable singletons for caching"
    And the approve dialog opens
    And Marcus clicks "Approve as Active" without editing
    Then the learning status changes to "active"
    And a success toast shows "Learning approved and activated"
    And the card is removed from the pending list
    And the Pending tab count updates to "(2)"
    And the Active tab count increments

  Scenario: Approve with text edit
    Given Marcus clicks "Approve" on "Log all errors with structured context"
    And the approve dialog opens with editable text
    When Marcus edits the text to "Log all errors with structured context including timestamp and request ID"
    And Marcus clicks "Approve as Active"
    Then the learning is saved with the edited text
    And the learning status changes to "active"
    And the active learnings list shows the updated text

  Scenario: Collision warning during approval
    Given there is an active learning "Do NOT use module-level mutable singletons for caching or shared state"
    When Marcus clicks "Approve" on "Avoid module-level mutable singletons for caching"
    Then the approve dialog shows a collision warning
    And the warning displays the similar active learning text
    And the warning shows similarity: 92%
    And Marcus can still choose to approve or cancel

  Scenario: Dismiss a pending learning with reason
    Given Marcus is on the Pending tab
    When Marcus clicks "Dismiss" on "Use RecordId objects instead of string IDs"
    And the dismiss dialog opens
    And Marcus enters reason "Too specific to current codebase, not a general rule"
    And Marcus clicks "Dismiss"
    Then the learning status changes to "dismissed"
    And a success toast shows "Learning dismissed"
    And the card is removed from the pending list
    And the reason is stored with the learning record

  Scenario: Dismiss requires a reason
    Given Marcus clicks "Dismiss" on a pending learning
    When the dismiss dialog opens
    Then the reason field is required
    And the "Dismiss" button is disabled until a reason is entered

  Scenario: Cancel approval returns to pending list
    Given Marcus opens the approve dialog for a pending learning
    When Marcus clicks "Cancel"
    Then the dialog closes
    And the learning remains in pending status
    And no changes are made

  Scenario: Undo approval via toast
    Given Marcus just approved a learning
    And the success toast is visible with an "Undo" action
    When Marcus clicks "Undo" within the toast timeout
    Then the learning reverts to "pending_approval" status
    And the pending count re-increments

content.text: Now I'll update US-LL-02 to reflect that triage lives primarily in the feed, with the library just showing pending items inline with the same actions.

content:
yes this sounds good

The feed is the notification layer — you see a pending learning, approve it, move on. The library is the management layer — you browse all learnings, filter by agent type, see dismissed ones, edit active ones, understand the full picture.

Practically this means:

US-LL-02 (Triage Pending) gets simplified — the library just shows pending learnings in the filtered list with the same approve/dismiss actions, not a separate triage flow. It reuses the same API endpoint.
The feed remains the primary triage surface for new pending items.
The library adds value by showing pending items in context alongside active/dismissed/deactivated learnings.

file.content:
journey:
  name: "Review & Approve/Dismiss Pending Learning"
  goal: "Triage agent-suggested learnings efficiently while maintaining governance"
  persona: "Workspace owner receiving agent learning suggestions"
  traces_to_jobs:
    - "Job 4: Approval & Triage"
    - "Job 1: Visibility & Audit (pending count)"

  emotional_arc:
    start: "Responsible -- 'I need to review what agents are suggesting'"
    middle: "Evaluating -- 'Is this learning valid and safe to activate?'"
    end: "Decisive -- 'Approved or dismissed with confidence'"

steps:
  - id: 1
    name: "Notice Pending Count"
    action: "See badge on Pending tab or sidebar indicator"

    shared_artifacts:
      - name: pending_count
        source: "API: GET /api/workspaces/:workspaceId/learnings?status=pending_approval (count)"
        displayed_as: "number badge on Pending tab"
        consumers: ["sidebar badge", "tab label"]

    emotional_state:
      entry: "Neutral, browsing library"
      exit: "Responsible -- aware items need attention"

    gherkin: |
      Scenario: Pending count visible on tab
        Given Marcus navigates to the learning library
        And 3 learnings have status "pending_approval"
        Then the Pending tab shows count "(3)"

  - id: 2
    name: "View Pending Learning Detail"
    action: "Read pending learning card with context"

    shared_artifacts:
      - name: pending_learning
        source: "API response: learning record with status=pending_approval"
        displayed_as: "card with text, source agent, confidence score, creation date"
        consumers: ["pending card view", "approve dialog", "dismiss dialog"]
      - name: suggested_by
        source: "learning record field: suggested_by"
        displayed_as: "agent name on card"
        consumers: ["card display"]
      - name: pattern_confidence
        source: "learning record field: pattern_confidence"
        displayed_as: "percentage on card"
        consumers: ["card display"]

    emotional_state:
      entry: "Responsible"
      exit: "Evaluating -- considering the suggestion's merit"

    gherkin: |
      Scenario: View pending learning with full context
        Given Marcus is on the Pending tab
        When Marcus views the pending learning "Avoid module-level mutable singletons"
        Then the card shows suggesting agent: "observer_agent"
        And the card shows confidence: 87%
        And the card shows creation date
        And Approve and Dismiss buttons are visible

  - id: 3
    name: "Approve Learning (with optional edit)"
    action: "Click Approve, optionally edit text, confirm"

    shared_artifacts:
      - name: collision_results
        source: "API: POST /api/workspaces/:workspaceId/learnings/:id/actions (collision check on approve)"
        displayed_as: "inline warning banner if collisions detected"
        consumers: ["approve dialog collision warning"]
      - name: edited_text
        source: "user input in approve dialog textarea"
        displayed_as: "editable text field"
        consumers: ["API: new_text field in approve action"]

    emotional_state:
      entry: "Evaluating"
      exit: "Confident -- approved with full understanding"

    gherkin: |
      Scenario: Approve pending learning
        Given Marcus is viewing the approve dialog for "Avoid module-level mutable singletons"
        And no collisions are detected
        When Marcus clicks "Approve as Active"
        Then the learning status changes to "active"
        And a success toast confirms "Learning approved and activated"
        And the pending count decrements by 1

      Scenario: Approve with edit
        Given Marcus is viewing the approve dialog
        When Marcus edits the text to add clarification
        And clicks "Approve as Active"
        Then the learning is saved with the edited text
        And the learning status changes to "active"

      Scenario: Collision warning shown during approval
        Given the pending learning is similar to an existing active learning
        When Marcus opens the approve dialog
        Then a collision warning banner is displayed
        And the warning shows the similar existing learning text
        And the warning shows the similarity percentage

  - id: 4
    name: "Dismiss Learning"
    action: "Click Dismiss, provide reason, confirm"

    shared_artifacts:
      - name: dismiss_reason
        source: "user input in dismiss dialog"
        displayed_as: "required text field"
        consumers: ["API: reason field in dismiss action"]

    emotional_state:
      entry: "Evaluating"
      exit: "Deliberate -- dismissed with documented reasoning"

    gherkin: |
      Scenario: Dismiss pending learning with reason
        Given Marcus is viewing the pending learning "Use RecordId objects not string IDs"
        When Marcus clicks Dismiss
        And enters reason "Too specific to current codebase, not a general rule"
        And confirms the dismissal
        Then the learning status changes to "dismissed"
        And a success toast confirms "Learning dismissed"
        And the pending count decrements by 1
        And the reason is stored in the learning record

integration_validation:
  shared_artifact_consistency:
    - artifact: pending_count
      must_match_across: [1, 3, 4]
      failure_message: "Pending count must update immediately after approve or dismiss"
    - artifact: pending_learning
      must_match_across: [2, 3, 4]
      failure_message: "Learning data in cards must match data shown in approve/dismiss dialogs"

|-------|-----------|-----------|-----------|--------|

| US-LL-03: Edit/Deactivate | PASSED | 2 | 5 | Must Have |
| US-LL-04: Create Learning | PASSED | 1-2 | 5 | Must Have |

Total: 7-9 days, 22 scenarios, all stories pass DoR.

## Backend Gaps Identified

These are not blockers for DoR but should be resolved before or during implementation:

1. **AGENT_TYPES shared constant** -- agent type list is scattered across server modules. Need a single source for UI filter dropdowns and targeting checkboxes.
2. **Edit priority/target_agents on active learnings** -- current action endpoint only supports status transitions and text updates via approve. A PUT endpoint or expanded action may be needed for editing priority and target_agents fields.
3. **Collision query for approve flow** -- collision detection runs during creation but may need to be queried separately when approving a pending learning.
4. **Reactivation transition** -- `deactivated -> active` is not currently a valid transition. If reactivation is desired, a new transition must be added.

## Recommended Implementation Order

1. US-LL-01 (Browse & Filter) -- foundation for all other stories
2. US-LL-04 (Create Learning) -- simpler dialog, establishes form patterns
3. US-LL-02 (Triage Pending) -- builds on card pattern, adds approve/dismiss dialogs
4. US-LL-03 (Edit/Deactivate) -- most backend gaps, benefit from patterns established in 02/04

content.text: Now I have all the content. Let me update the artifacts to reflect the simplified approach — triage stays in the feed, library shows pending inline with the same actions.

input.content:
# US-LL-02: Inline Pending Actions in Library

## Problem

The governance feed already serves as the primary triage surface for pending learnings — agent-suggested learnings appear as "Needs Review" cards with Approve/Dismiss/Discuss actions. However, when a user is browsing the learning library (US-LL-01), pending learnings appear in the filtered list without any way to act on them. The user must context-switch to the feed to approve or dismiss. The library should offer the same inline actions so users can triage pending items in context alongside active/dismissed/deactivated learnings.

## Who

- Workspace owner | Browsing the learning library | Encounters pending learnings in the list

## Solution

Pending learning cards in the library list include inline Approve and Dismiss action buttons — the same actions available in the governance feed. Clicking Approve opens a lightweight dialog with optional text editing and collision warnings. Clicking Dismiss opens a dialog requiring a reason. No dedicated "Pending" tab or separate triage flow — pending learnings are just another status in the existing filter.

**Key design decision:** The feed is the notification layer (push — "something needs attention"). The library is the management layer (pull — "let me review everything"). Both surfaces share the same action endpoints and dialog patterns.

## Job Story Trace

- **Job 4: Approval & Triage** — "When agents suggest learnings from patterns they detect, I want to review and approve or dismiss them efficiently, so I maintain governance without bottlenecking."
- **Job 1: Visibility & Audit** — "When browsing all learnings, I want to see pending items in context alongside active ones, so I understand the full picture."

## Domain Examples

### 1: Happy Path — Approve while browsing

Marcus is browsing the library filtered to "All Statuses" for agent type "mcp". He sees 12 active learnings and 1 pending learning intermixed (sorted by creation date). The pending card shows a "pending" badge, the suggesting agent (observer_agent), and confidence (87%). He clicks Approve directly on the card. A compact dialog appears with editable text and no collisions. He clicks "Approve as Active." Toast confirms. The card's badge updates to "active" in place.

### 2: Edge Case — Dismiss while filtered to pending only

Marcus filters the library to status "pending_approval". Three items appear. He reads each one in context — seeing their type, priority, and target agents alongside the filter. He dismisses one with reason "Already covered by CLAUDE.md guidelines." The card is removed from the filtered view. Count updates.

### 3: Boundary — Collision warning on approve

Marcus clicks Approve on a pending learning "Use RecordId objects instead of string IDs." The approve dialog shows a collision warning: similar active learning exists (94% similarity). Marcus reads both, decides this is a duplicate, and clicks Cancel. He then dismisses it with reason "Duplicate of existing active learning."

## UAT Scenarios (BDD)

### Scenario: Pending learning cards show inline actions

```gherkin
Given Marcus navigates to the learning library
And the workspace has 1 pending learning and 5 active learnings
When Marcus views the unfiltered list
Then the pending learning card shows "Approve" and "Dismiss" buttons
And active learning cards do not show "Approve" or "Dismiss" buttons
And the pending card displays a "pending" status badge
```

### Scenario: Approve from library inline

```gherkin
Given Marcus is viewing the learning library
And a pending learning "Avoid module-level mutable singletons" is visible
When Marcus clicks "Approve" on that card
Then an approve dialog opens with editable text
And Marcus clicks "Approve as Active"
Then the learning status changes to "active"
And a success toast shows "Learning approved and activated"
And the card's status badge updates to "active" in place
```

### Scenario: Approve with text edit from library

```gherkin
Given Marcus opens the approve dialog for a pending learning
When Marcus edits the text to add clarification
And clicks "Approve as Active"
Then the learning is saved with the edited text
And the card updates to show the new text and "active" status
```

### Scenario: Dismiss from library with reason

```gherkin
Given Marcus clicks "Dismiss" on a pending learning in the library
Then a dismiss dialog opens with a required reason field
When Marcus enters reason "Already covered by CLAUDE.md"
And clicks "Dismiss"
Then the learning status changes to "dismissed"
And a success toast shows "Learning dismissed"
```

### Scenario: Collision warning in approve dialog

```gherkin
Given a pending learning is similar to an existing active learning
When Marcus clicks "Approve" on the pending learning
Then the approve dialog shows a collision warning
And the warning displays the similar active learning text and similarity percentage
And Marcus can choose to approve anyway or cancel
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Pending learning cards in the library list show "Approve" and "Dismiss" inline action buttons
- [ ] Only pending cards show triage actions — active/dismissed/deactivated cards do not
- [ ] Approve action opens dialog with editable text field (pre-filled with current text)
- [ ] Collision warnings display inline in approve dialog when similar active learnings exist
- [ ] Approve-with-edit sends updated text via `new_text` field in API action
- [ ] Dismiss action requires a non-empty reason before submission
- [ ] Card updates optimistically after successful action (status badge, button visibility)
- [ ] Success toast shown after approve and dismiss actions

## Technical Notes

- Reuses the same API endpoints as the governance feed:
  - Approve: `POST /api/workspaces/:workspaceId/learnings/:id/actions` with `{ action: "approve", new_text?: string }`
  - Dismiss: same path with `{ action: "dismiss", reason: string }`
- The approve/dismiss dialog components should be shared between feed and library (extract if not already shared)
- Collision detection for approve flow: may need a separate client-side fetch or server-side inclusion in the action response
- No dedicated Pending tab — pending is a status filter value in US-LL-01's filter controls

## Dependencies

- US-LL-01 (Browse & Filter Library) — pending learnings appear in the library list
- Existing action API endpoint (complete)
- Governance feed approve/dismiss UI (complete — dialog components may be extractable)

## Solution

Pending learning cards in the library list include inline Approve and Dismiss action buttons — the same actions available in the governance feed. Clicking Approve opens a lightweight dialog with optional text editing and collision warnings. Clicking Dismiss opens a dialog requiring a reason. No dedicated "Pending" tab or separate triage flow — pending learnings are just another status in the existing filter.

**Key design decision:** The feed is the notification layer (push — "something needs attention"). The library is the management layer (pull — "let me review everything"). Both surfaces share the same action endpoints and dialog patterns.

## Job Story Trace

- **Job 4: Approval & Triage** — "When agents suggest learnings from patterns they detect, I want to review and approve or dismiss them efficiently, so I maintain governance without bottlenecking."
- **Job 1: Visibility & Audit** — "When browsing all learnings, I want to see pending items in context alongside active ones, so I understand the full picture."

## Domain Examples

### 1: Happy Path — Approve while browsing

Marcus is browsing the library filtered to "All Statuses" for agent type "mcp". He sees 12 active learnings and 1 pending learning intermixed (sorted by creation date). The pending card shows a "pending" badge, the suggesting agent (observer_agent), and confidence (87%). He clicks Approve directly on the card. A compact dialog appears with editable text and no collisions. He clicks "Approve as Active." Toast confirms. The card's badge updates to "active" in place.

### 2: Edge Case — Dismiss while filtered to pending only

Marcus filters the library to status "pending_approval". Three items appear. He reads each one in context — seeing their type, priority, and target agents alongside the filter. He dismisses one with reason "Already covered by CLAUDE.md guidelines." The card is removed from the filtered view. Count updates.

### 3: Boundary — Collision warning on approve

Marcus clicks Approve on a pending learning "Use RecordId objects instead of string IDs." The approve dialog shows a collision warning: similar active learning exists (94% similarity). Marcus reads both, decides this is a duplicate, and clicks Cancel. He then dismisses it with reason "Duplicate of existing active learning."

## UAT Scenarios (BDD)

### Scenario: Pending learning cards show inline actions

```gherkin
Given Marcus navigates to the learning library
And the workspace has 1 pending learning and 5 active learnings
When Marcus views the unfiltered list
Then the pending learning card shows "Approve" and "Dismiss" buttons
And active learning cards do not show "Approve" or "Dismiss" buttons
And the pending card displays a "pending" status badge
```

### Scenario: Approve from library inline

```gherkin
Given Marcus is viewing the learning library
And a pending learning "Avoid module-level mutable singletons" is visible
When Marcus clicks "Approve" on that card
Then an approve dialog opens with editable text
And Marcus clicks "Approve as Active"
Then the learning status changes to "active"
And a success toast shows "Learning approved and activated"
And the card's status badge updates to "active" in place
```

### Scenario: Approve with text edit from library

```gherkin
Given Marcus opens the approve dialog for a pending learning
When Marcus edits the text to add clarification
And clicks "Approve as Active"
Then the learning is saved with the edited text
And the card updates to show the new text and "active" status
```

### Scenario: Dismiss from library with reason

```gherkin
Given Marcus clicks "Dismiss" on a pending learning in the library
Then a dismiss dialog opens with a required reason field
When Marcus enters reason "Already covered by CLAUDE.md"
And clicks "Dismiss"
Then the learning status changes to "dismissed"
And a success toast shows "Learning dismissed"
```

### Scenario: Collision warning in approve dialog

```gherkin
Given a pending learning is similar to an existing active learning
When Marcus clicks "Approve" on the pending learning
Then the approve dialog shows a collision warning
And the warning displays the similar active learning text and similarity percentage
And Marcus can choose to approve anyway or cancel
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Pending learning cards in the library list show "Approve" and "Dismiss" inline action buttons
- [ ] Only pending cards show triage actions — active/dismissed/deactivated cards do not
- [ ] Approve action opens dialog with editable text field (pre-filled with current text)
- [ ] Collision warnings display inline in approve dialog when similar active learnings exist
- [ ] Approve-with-edit sends updated text via `new_text` field in API action
- [ ] Dismiss action requires a non-empty reason before submission
- [ ] Card updates optimistically after successful action (status badge, button visibility)
- [ ] Success toast shown after approve and dismiss actions

## Technical Notes

- Reuses the same API endpoints as the governance feed:
  - Approve: `POST /api/workspaces/:workspaceId/learnings/:id/actions` with `{ action: "approve", new_text?: string }`
  - Dismiss: same path with `{ action: "dismiss", reason: string }`
- The approve/dismiss dialog components should be shared between feed and library (extract if not already shared)
- Collision detection for approve flow: may need a separate client-side fetch or server-side inclusion in the action response
- No dedicated Pending tab — pending is a status filter value in US-LL-01's filter controls

## Dependencies

- US-LL-01 (Browse & Filter Library) — pending learnings appear in the library list
- Existing action API endpoint (complete)
- Governance feed approve/dismiss UI (complete — dialog components may be extractable)

# US-LL-02: Review & Approve/Dismiss Pending Learnings

## Problem

Marcus is a workspace owner whose AI agents (observer, PM) suggest behavioral learnings from patterns they detect during work sessions. These suggestions accumulate as "pending_approval" records with no UI to review them. Marcus has no idea suggestions exist unless he queries the API. Agent-suggested learnings pile up, creating governance debt -- valuable patterns go unreviewed and bad suggestions linger indefinitely. The rate limiter caps at 5 per agent per week, but even those 5 are invisible.

## Who

- Workspace owner | Receiving agent learning suggestions | Wants efficient governance without bottlenecking

## Solution

A triage workflow on the Pending tab of the Learning Library. Each pending learning card shows the suggesting agent, confidence score, and creation date, with inline Approve and Dismiss actions. Approve opens a dialog where Marcus can optionally edit the text before activation. Dismiss requires a reason. Collision warnings surface when the pending learning overlaps with existing active learnings.

## Job Story Trace

- **Job 4: Approval & Triage** -- "When agents suggest learnings from patterns they detect, I want to review and approve or dismiss them efficiently, so I maintain governance without bottlenecking."

## Domain Examples

### 1: Happy Path -- Marcus approves a high-confidence suggestion

The observer_agent suggested "Avoid module-level mutable singletons for caching" with 87% confidence after detecting the pattern in 3 coding sessions. Marcus navigates to the Pending tab, sees 3 items. He clicks Approve on this learning. The approve dialog shows the text (editable), type (instruction), priority (medium), and agents (mcp). No collisions detected. Marcus clicks "Approve as Active." Toast confirms "Learning approved and activated." Pending count drops from 3 to 2.

### 2: Edge Case -- Approve with edit and collision warning

The pm_agent suggested "Use RecordId objects instead of string IDs in queries" with 92% confidence. Marcus clicks Approve. The approve dialog shows a collision warning: "Similar to active learning: 'Do NOT use string-based record IDs like table:id in queries' (similarity: 94%)." Marcus reads both, decides the new one is a better phrasing. He edits the text to "Always use RecordId objects from the surrealdb SDK, never raw table:id strings, in all database queries." Clicks "Approve as Active." The old learning remains active -- Marcus can deactivate it separately if desired.

### 3: Error/Boundary -- Dismiss with required reason

Marcus sees "Log all errors with structured context" suggested by observer_agent with 78% confidence. He considers it too vague. He clicks Dismiss. The dismiss dialog requires a reason. He types "Too vague -- needs specifics about what structured context means. Agents already follow CLAUDE.md error handling guidelines." Clicks Dismiss. The learning moves to the Dismissed tab with the reason preserved.

## UAT Scenarios (BDD)

### Scenario: View pending learnings queue

```gherkin
Given Marcus navigates to the learning library
And the workspace has 3 pending learnings
When Marcus clicks the "Pending (3)" tab
Then 3 pending learning cards are displayed
And each card shows: text, suggesting agent, confidence score, creation date
And each card has "Approve" and "Dismiss" buttons
```

### Scenario: Approve without edits

```gherkin
Given Marcus is on the Pending tab
When Marcus clicks "Approve" on "Avoid module-level mutable singletons for caching"
And the approve dialog opens with no collisions
And Marcus clicks "Approve as Active"
Then the learning status changes to "active"
And a success toast shows "Learning approved and activated"
And the Pending tab count updates to "(2)"
And the Active tab count increments
```

### Scenario: Approve with text edit

```gherkin
Given Marcus opens the approve dialog for "Log all errors with structured context"
When Marcus edits the text to "Log all errors with structured context including timestamp, request ID, and error code"
And Marcus clicks "Approve as Active"
Then the learning is saved with the edited text
And the learning appears in the Active tab with the updated text
```

### Scenario: Collision warning during approval

```gherkin
Given there is an active learning "Do NOT use string-based record IDs"
And Marcus opens the approve dialog for "Use RecordId objects instead of string IDs"
Then the dialog shows a collision warning
And the warning displays the similar active learning text
And the warning shows similarity percentage: 94%
And Marcus can choose to approve or cancel
```

### Scenario: Dismiss with required reason

```gherkin
Given Marcus clicks "Dismiss" on a pending learning
Then a dismiss dialog opens with a required reason field
And the "Dismiss" button is disabled until a reason is entered
When Marcus enters reason "Too vague -- needs specifics about structured context"
And Marcus clicks "Dismiss"
Then the learning status changes to "dismissed"
And the dismiss reason is stored with the learning record
And a success toast shows "Learning dismissed"
```

### Scenario: Cancel returns to pending list

```gherkin
Given Marcus has opened the approve dialog for a pending learning
When Marcus clicks "Cancel"
Then the dialog closes
And the learning remains in "pending_approval" status
And the pending count is unchanged
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Pending tab shows learning cards with: text, suggesting agent name, confidence score, creation date
- [ ] Approve action opens dialog with editable text, type, priority, and agent targeting
- [ ] Collision warnings display inline in approve dialog when similar active learnings exist
- [ ] Approve-with-edit sends updated text via `new_text` field in API action
- [ ] Dismiss action requires a non-empty reason before submission
- [ ] Tab counts update immediately after approve/dismiss (optimistic update)
- [ ] Card is removed from pending list after successful action
- [ ] Success toast shown after approve and dismiss actions

## Technical Notes

- Approve endpoint: `POST /api/workspaces/:workspaceId/learnings/:id/actions` with `{ action: "approve", new_text?: string }`
- Dismiss endpoint: same path with `{ action: "dismiss", reason: string }`
- Collision detection runs server-side during creation -- for the approve flow, collisions may need to be fetched client-side or included in list response
- The `pattern_confidence` and `suggested_by` fields are available in the list API response
- Undo for approve/dismiss is a nice-to-have but not required for MVP (no API endpoint for status revert from active/dismissed)

## Dependencies

- US-LL-01 (Browse & Filter Library) -- the pending tab is part of the library page
- Existing action API endpoint (complete)
- Collision detection API (exists for creation, may need client-side query for approve flow)

input.content:
# Journey: Inline Pending Actions in Library

## Design Decision

The governance feed is the **notification layer** (push) for pending learnings. The library is the **management layer** (pull). Both surfaces offer the same approve/dismiss actions via shared dialog components.

## Emotional Arc

```
Start: Browsing               Middle: Noticing              End: Acting
"I'm reviewing all            "This one is pending,         "Approved/dismissed
 my learnings"                 let me handle it now"          without leaving"
```

## Flow: Approve Inline

```
+-- Learning Library (unfiltered) ---------------------------------+
|                                                                   |
|  Status: [All v]  Type: [All v]  Agent: [All v]                  |
|                                                                   |
|  +-------------------------------------------------------------+ |
|  | "Always use RecordId objects from surrealdb SDK"             | |
|  | [active] constraint | high | mcp, chat_agent                | |
|  +-------------------------------------------------------------+ |
|                                                                   |
|  +-------------------------------------------------------------+ |
|  | "Avoid module-level mutable singletons for caching"          | |
|  | [pending] instruction | medium | mcp                         | |
|  | suggested by: observer_agent | confidence: 87%               | |
|  |                                     [Approve] [Dismiss]      | |
|  +-------------------------------------------------------------+ |
|                                                                   |
|  +-------------------------------------------------------------+ |
|  | "Use snake_case for database field names"                    | |
|  | [active] instruction | low | All agents                     | |
|  +-------------------------------------------------------------+ |
+-------------------------------------------------------------------+
        |
        | (click Approve)
        v
+-- Approve Dialog ------------------------------------------------+
|                                                                   |
|  Approve Learning                                                 |
|                                                                   |
|  +-- Text (editable) ----------------------------------------+  |
|  | Avoid module-level mutable singletons for caching.         |  |
|  +------------------------------------------------------------+  |
|                                                                   |
|  Type: instruction    Priority: [medium v]                        |
|  Applies to: [mcp]                                                |
|                                                                   |
|  (collision warning appears here if detected)                     |
|                                                                   |
|                           [Cancel]  [Approve as Active]           |
+-------------------------------------------------------------------+
        |
        | (click Approve as Active)
        v
+-- Toast ---------------------------------------------------------+
|  Learning approved and activated.                                 |
+-------------------------------------------------------------------+
        |
        v
(Card badge updates from [pending] to [active], action buttons removed)
```

## Flow: Dismiss Inline

```
+-- Pending card with Dismiss clicked -----------------------------+
|                                                                   |
|  Dismiss Learning                                                 |
|                                                                   |
|  "Use RecordId objects instead of string IDs in queries"          |
|                                                                   |
|  Reason (required):                                               |
|  +------------------------------------------------------------+  |
|  | Already covered by existing active learning                 |  |
|  +------------------------------------------------------------+  |
|                                                                   |
|                           [Cancel]  [Dismiss]                     |
+-------------------------------------------------------------------+
        |
        | (click Dismiss)
        v
+-- Toast ---------------------------------------------------------+
|  Learning dismissed.                                              |
+-------------------------------------------------------------------+
        |
        v
(Card badge updates to [dismissed], or removed if filtered to non-dismissed)
```

## Emotional Annotations

| Step | Entry Emotion | Exit Emotion | Design Lever |
|------|---------------|--------------|--------------|
| Browsing library, see pending card | Neutral | Aware | Pending badge + action buttons make it obvious without creating urgency |
| Read pending card context | Aware | Evaluating | Source agent + confidence shown inline — no need to open a detail view |
| Click Approve | Evaluating | Confident | Dialog allows optional edit; collision warning is informational, not blocking |
| Click Dismiss | Evaluating | Deliberate | Reason field ensures thoughtful dismissals |
| See toast confirmation | Acting | Satisfied | Card updates in place — no page reload or navigation |

## Key Difference from Feed

| Aspect | Feed | Library |
|--------|------|---------|
| Discovery | Push — pending card appears in "Needs Review" | Pull — user filters/browses and encounters pending items |
| Context | Isolated card among other governance items | In context alongside active/dismissed learnings |
| After action | Card removed from feed | Card updates in place (badge changes) |
| Primary use | "What needs my attention?" | "What's the full picture?" |

```
|  +------------------------------------------------------------+  |
|                                                                   |
|                           [Cancel]  [Dismiss]                     |
+-------------------------------------------------------------------+
        |
        | (click Dismiss)
        v
+-- Toast ---------------------------------------------------------+
|  Learning dismissed.                                              |
+-------------------------------------------------------------------+
        |
        v
(Card badge updates to [dismissed], or removed if filtered to non-dismissed)
```

## Emotional Annotations

| Step | Entry Emotion | Exit Emotion | Design Lever |
|------|---------------|--------------|--------------|
| Browsing library, see pending card | Neutral | Aware | Pending badge + action buttons make it obvious without creating urgency |
| Read pending card context | Aware | Evaluating | Source agent + confidence shown inline — no need to open a detail view |
| Click Approve | Evaluating | Confident | Dialog allows optional edit; collision warning is informational, not blocking |
| Click Dismiss | Evaluating | Deliberate | Reason field ensures thoughtful dismissals |
| See toast confirmation | Acting | Satisfied | Card updates in place — no page reload or navigation |

## Key Difference from Feed

| Aspect | Feed | Library |
|--------|------|---------|
| Discovery | Push — pending card appears in "Needs Review" | Pull — user filters/browses and encounters pending items |
| Context | Isolated card among other governance items | In context alongside active/dismissed learnings |
| After action | Card removed from feed | Card updates in place (badge changes) |
| Primary use | "What needs my attention?" | "What's the full picture?" |

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Journey: Review & Approve/Dismiss Pending Learning

## Emotional Arc

```
Start: Responsible       Middle: Evaluating       End: Decisive
"I need to review        "Is this learning        "Approved/dismissed
 what agents suggest"     valid and safe?"          with confidence"
```

## Flow: Approve

```
+-- Pending Tab (3 items) ------------------------------------------+
|                                                                    |
|  [Active (12)] [Pending (3)] [Dismissed] [Deactivated]            |
|                        ^^^                                         |
|  +--------------------------------------------------------------+ |
|  | "Avoid module-level mutable singletons for caching"           | |
|  | instruction | medium | mcp | suggested by: observer_agent     | |
|  | Confidence: 0.87 | Created 2026-03-12                         | |
|  |                                      [Approve] [Dismiss]      | |
|  +--------------------------------------------------------------+ |
|                                                                    |
+--------------------------------------------------------------------+
         |
         | (click Approve)
         v
+-- Approve Dialog -------------------------------------------------+
|                                                                    |
|  Approve Learning                                                  |
|                                                                    |
|  "Avoid module-level mutable singletons for caching"               |
|                                                                    |
|  +-- Edit text (optional) ------------------------------------+   |
|  | Avoid module-level mutable singletons for caching.          |   |
|  | Module-level state is shared across the entire process.     |   |
|  +-------------------------------------------------------------+  |
|                                                                    |
|  Type: instruction    Priority: [medium v]                         |
|  Applies to: [mcp]                                                 |
|                                                                    |
|  ! Collision warning:                                              |
|  | Similar to active learning: "Do NOT use module-level            |
|  | mutable singletons..." (92% similarity)                         |
|  | Action: This may be a duplicate. Consider dismissing.           |
|                                                                    |
|                            [Cancel]  [Approve as Active]           |
+--------------------------------------------------------------------+
         |
         | (click Approve as Active)
         v
+-- Success Toast ---------------------------------------------------+
|  Learning approved and activated.                      [Undo]      |
+--------------------------------------------------------------------+
         |
         v
(Pending count decrements to 2, card removed from list)
```

## Flow: Dismiss

```
+-- Pending card with Dismiss clicked ------------------------------+
|                                                                    |
|  Dismiss Learning                                                  |
|                                                                    |
|  "Use RecordId objects instead of string IDs in queries"           |
|                                                                    |
|  Reason (required):                                                |
|  +-------------------------------------------------------------+  |
|  | Too specific to current codebase, not a general rule        |   |
|  +-------------------------------------------------------------+  |
|                                                                    |
|                            [Cancel]  [Dismiss]                     |
+--------------------------------------------------------------------+
         |
         | (click Dismiss)
         v
+-- Success Toast ---------------------------------------------------+
|  Learning dismissed.                                   [Undo]      |
+--------------------------------------------------------------------+
```

## Emotional Annotations

| Step | Entry Emotion | Exit Emotion | Design Lever |
|------|---------------|--------------|--------------|
| See pending badge/tab | Responsible | Curious | Badge count creates gentle urgency without alarm |
| Read pending learning text | Curious | Evaluating | Show full context: source agent, confidence, creation date |
| See collision warning | Evaluating | Cautious | Warning is informational, not blocking -- user decides |
| Click Approve | Cautious | Confident | Approve dialog allows editing before activation |
| See success toast | Confident | Satisfied | Immediate confirmation, undo available |
| Click Dismiss | Evaluating | Deliberate | Reason field ensures dismissals are thoughtful |

input.content:
journey:
  name: "Inline Pending Actions in Library"
  goal: "Act on pending learnings without leaving the library"
  persona: "Workspace owner browsing the learning library"
  traces_to_jobs:
    - "Job 4: Approval & Triage"
    - "Job 1: Visibility & Audit"

  design_decision: |
    Feed = notification layer (push). Library = management layer (pull).
    Both share the same approve/dismiss dialogs and API endpoints.
    No dedicated Pending tab — pending is a status filter value.

  emotional_arc:
    start: "Browsing — reviewing all learnings in the library"
    middle: "Noticing — a pending item stands out with action buttons"
    end: "Acting — approved or dismissed without context-switching"

steps:
  - id: 1
    name: "Encounter Pending Card in List"
    action: "See pending learning card with action buttons while browsing"

    shared_artifacts:
      - name: pending_learning
        source: "API: GET /api/workspaces/:workspaceId/learnings (status=pending_approval in results)"
        displayed_as: "card with pending badge, text, source agent, confidence, action buttons"
        consumers: ["library list", "approve dialog", "dismiss dialog"]

    emotional_state:
      entry: "Neutral — browsing the library"
      exit: "Aware — this item needs a decision"

    gherkin: |
      Scenario: Pending cards show inline actions
        Given the learning library shows learnings of mixed statuses
        When a pending learning card appears in the list
        Then it displays a "pending" status badge
        And it shows "Approve" and "Dismiss" action buttons
        And it shows the suggesting agent name and confidence score
        And active/dismissed/deactivated cards do NOT show triage buttons

  - id: 2
    name: "Approve Learning Inline"
    action: "Click Approve on pending card, optionally edit, confirm"

    shared_artifacts:
      - name: collision_results
        source: "Server-side collision check during approve"
        displayed_as: "inline warning in approve dialog"
        consumers: ["approve dialog"]
      - name: edited_text
        source: "User input in approve dialog textarea"
        consumers: ["API: new_text field in approve action"]

    emotional_state:
      entry: "Evaluating"
      exit: "Confident — approved with understanding"

    gherkin: |
      Scenario: Approve pending learning from library
        Given Marcus clicks "Approve" on a pending learning card
        Then an approve dialog opens with editable text
        When Marcus clicks "Approve as Active"
        Then the learning status changes to "active"
        And a success toast confirms activation
        And the card's badge updates to "active" in place

      Scenario: Approve with text edit
        Given the approve dialog is open
        When Marcus edits the text and clicks "Approve as Active"
        Then the learning is saved with the edited text

      Scenario: Collision warning in approve dialog
        Given the pending learning is similar to an existing active learning
        When Marcus opens the approve dialog
        Then a collision warning shows the similar learning and similarity %

  - id: 3
    name: "Dismiss Learning Inline"
    action: "Click Dismiss on pending card, provide reason, confirm"

    shared_artifacts:
      - name: dismiss_reason
        source: "User input in dismiss dialog"
        consumers: ["API: reason field in dismiss action"]

    emotional_state:
      entry: "Evaluating"
      exit: "Deliberate — dismissed with documented reasoning"

    gherkin: |
      Scenario: Dismiss pending learning from library
        Given Marcus clicks "Dismiss" on a pending learning card
        Then a dismiss dialog opens with a required reason field
        When Marcus enters a reason and clicks "Dismiss"
        Then the learning status changes to "dismissed"
        And a success toast confirms dismissal

integration_validation:
  shared_artifact_consistency:
    - artifact: pending_learning
      must_match_across: [1, 2, 3]
      failure_message: "Learning data in list cards must match data in approve/dismiss dialogs"
  shared_components:
    - component: "approve dialog"
      shared_between: ["governance feed", "learning library"]
      note: "Extract as shared component if not already"
    - component: "dismiss dialog"
      shared_between: ["governance feed", "learning library"]
      note: "Extract as shared component if not already"

toolUseResult.originalFile:
journey:
  name: "Review & Approve/Dismiss Pending Learning"
  goal: "Triage agent-suggested learnings efficiently while maintaining governance"
  persona: "Workspace owner receiving agent learning suggestions"
  traces_to_jobs:
    - "Job 4: Approval & Triage"
    - "Job 1: Visibility & Audit (pending count)"

  emotional_arc:
    start: "Responsible -- 'I need to review what agents are suggesting'"
    middle: "Evaluating -- 'Is this learning valid and safe to activate?'"
    end: "Decisive -- 'Approved or dismissed with confidence'"

steps:
  - id: 1
    name: "Notice Pending Count"
    action: "See badge on Pending tab or sidebar indicator"

    shared_artifacts:
      - name: pending_count
        source: "API: GET /api/workspaces/:workspaceId/learnings?status=pending_approval (count)"
        displayed_as: "number badge on Pending tab"
        consumers: ["sidebar badge", "tab label"]

    emotional_state:
      entry: "Neutral, browsing library"
      exit: "Responsible -- aware items need attention"

    gherkin: |
      Scenario: Pending count visible on tab
        Given Marcus navigates to the learning library
        And 3 learnings have status "pending_approval"
        Then the Pending tab shows count "(3)"

  - id: 2
    name: "View Pending Learning Detail"
    action: "Read pending learning card with context"

    shared_artifacts:
      - name: pending_learning
        source: "API response: learning record with status=pending_approval"
        displayed_as: "card with text, source agent, confidence score, creation date"
        consumers: ["pending card view", "approve dialog", "dismiss dialog"]
      - name: suggested_by
        source: "learning record field: suggested_by"
        displayed_as: "agent name on card"
        consumers: ["card display"]
      - name: pattern_confidence
        source: "learning record field: pattern_confidence"
        displayed_as: "percentage on card"
        consumers: ["card display"]

    emotional_state:
      entry: "Responsible"
      exit: "Evaluating -- considering the suggestion's merit"

    gherkin: |
      Scenario: View pending learning with full context
        Given Marcus is on the Pending tab
        When Marcus views the pending learning "Avoid module-level mutable singletons"
        Then the card shows suggesting agent: "observer_agent"
        And the card shows confidence: 87%
        And the card shows creation date
        And Approve and Dismiss buttons are visible

  - id: 3
    name: "Approve Learning (with optional edit)"
    action: "Click Approve, optionally edit text, confirm"

    shared_artifacts:
      - name: collision_results
        source: "API: POST /api/workspaces/:workspaceId/learnings/:id/actions (collision check on approve)"
        displayed_as: "inline warning banner if collisions detected"
        consumers: ["approve dialog collision warning"]
      - name: edited_text
        source: "user input in approve dialog textarea"
        displayed_as: "editable text field"
        consumers: ["API: new_text field in approve action"]

    emotional_state:
      entry: "Evaluating"
      exit: "Confident -- approved with full understanding"

    gherkin: |
      Scenario: Approve pending learning
        Given Marcus is viewing the approve dialog for "Avoid module-level mutable singletons"
        And no collisions are detected
        When Marcus clicks "Approve as Active"
        Then the learning status changes to "active"
        And a success toast confirms "Learning approved and activated"
        And the pending count decrements by 1

      Scenario: Approve with edit
        Given Marcus is viewing the approve dialog
        When Marcus edits the text to add clarification
        And clicks "Approve as Active"
        Then the learning is saved with the edited text
        And the learning status changes to "active"

      Scenario: Collision warning shown during approval
        Given the pending learning is similar to an existing active learning
        When Marcus opens the approve dialog
        Then a collision warning banner is displayed
        And the warning shows the similar existing learning text
        And the warning shows the similarity percentage

  - id: 4
    name: "Dismiss Learning"
    action: "Click Dismiss, provide reason, confirm"

    shared_artifacts:
      - name: dismiss_reason
        source: "user input in dismiss dialog"
        displayed_as: "required text field"
        consumers: ["API: reason field in dismiss action"]

    emotional_state:
      entry: "Evaluating"
      exit: "Deliberate -- dismissed with documented reasoning"

    gherkin: |
      Scenario: Dismiss pending learning with reason
        Given Marcus is viewing the pending learning "Use RecordId objects not string IDs"
        When Marcus clicks Dismiss
        And enters reason "Too specific to current codebase, not a general rule"
        And confirms the dismissal
        Then the learning status changes to "dismissed"
        And a success toast confirms "Learning dismissed"
        And the pending count decrements by 1
        And the reason is stored in the learning record

integration_validation:
  shared_artifact_consistency:
    - artifact: pending_count
      must_match_across: [1, 3, 4]
      failure_message: "Pending count must update immediately after approve or dismiss"
    - artifact: pending_learning
      must_match_across: [2, 3, 4]
      failure_message: "Learning data in cards must match data shown in approve/dismiss dialogs"

input.content:
Feature: Inline Pending Actions in Learning Library
  As a workspace owner browsing the learning library,
  I want to approve or dismiss pending learnings without leaving the library,
  so I can triage in context alongside active and dismissed learnings.

  # Traces to: Job 4 (Approval & Triage), Job 1 (Visibility & Audit)
  # Design decision: Feed = push notification layer, Library = pull management layer.
  # Both share approve/dismiss dialog components and API endpoints.

  Background:
    Given Marcus is logged into workspace "brain-v1"
    And the workspace has the following learnings:
      | text                                              | status           | type        | suggested_by    | confidence |
      | Always use RecordId objects from surrealdb SDK     | active           | constraint  | -               | -          |
      | Avoid module-level mutable singletons for caching | pending_approval | instruction | observer_agent  | 0.87       |
      | Use snake_case for database field names            | active           | instruction | -               | -          |

  Scenario: Pending cards show inline action buttons
    Given Marcus navigates to the learning library
    When the unfiltered list is displayed
    Then the pending learning card shows "Approve" and "Dismiss" buttons
    And the pending card shows suggesting agent "observer_agent"
    And the pending card shows confidence "87%"
    And active learning cards do NOT show "Approve" or "Dismiss" buttons

  Scenario: Approve pending learning from library
    Given Marcus is viewing the learning library
    When Marcus clicks "Approve" on "Avoid module-level mutable singletons for caching"
    And the approve dialog opens with editable text
    And Marcus clicks "Approve as Active" without editing
    Then the learning status changes to "active"
    And a success toast shows "Learning approved and activated"
    And the card's status badge updates to "active" in place
    And the "Approve" and "Dismiss" buttons are removed from the card

  Scenario: Approve with text edit from library
    Given Marcus opens the approve dialog for "Avoid module-level mutable singletons for caching"
    When Marcus edits the text to "Avoid module-level mutable singletons for caching — use dependency injection instead"
    And Marcus clicks "Approve as Active"
    Then the learning is saved with the edited text
    And the card displays the updated text

  Scenario: Collision warning during library approval
    Given there is an active learning "Do NOT use module-level mutable singletons for caching or shared state"
    And Marcus clicks "Approve" on "Avoid module-level mutable singletons for caching"
    Then the approve dialog shows a collision warning
    And the warning displays the similar active learning text
    And the warning shows the similarity percentage
    And Marcus can choose to approve anyway or cancel

  Scenario: Dismiss pending learning from library with reason
    Given Marcus clicks "Dismiss" on "Avoid module-level mutable singletons for caching"
    Then a dismiss dialog opens with a required reason field
    And the "Dismiss" button is disabled until a reason is entered
    When Marcus enters reason "Already covered by CLAUDE.md guidelines"
    And Marcus clicks "Dismiss"
    Then the learning status changes to "dismissed"
    And a success toast shows "Learning dismissed"
    And the reason is stored with the learning record

  Scenario: Cancel dialog returns to library unchanged
    Given Marcus has opened the approve dialog for a pending learning
    When Marcus clicks "Cancel"
    Then the dialog closes
    And the learning remains in "pending_approval" status
    And no changes are made to the card

toolUseResult.originalFile:
Feature: Review & Approve/Dismiss Pending Learning
  As a workspace owner receiving agent learning suggestions,
  I want to review and approve or dismiss each suggestion efficiently,
  so I can maintain governance without bottlenecking the system.

  # Traces to: Job 4 (Approval & Triage)

  Background:
    Given Marcus is logged into workspace "brain-v1"
    And the workspace has the following pending learnings:
      | text                                              | type        | suggested_by    | confidence | agents          |
      | Avoid module-level mutable singletons for caching | instruction | observer_agent  | 0.87       | mcp             |
      | Use RecordId objects instead of string IDs         | constraint  | pm_agent        | 0.92       | mcp, chat_agent |
      | Log all errors with structured context            | instruction | observer_agent  | 0.78       | All agents      |

  Scenario: View pending learnings queue
    Given Marcus navigates to the learning library
    When Marcus clicks the "Pending (3)" tab
    Then 3 pending learning cards are displayed
    And each card shows the learning text
    And each card shows the suggesting agent name
    And each card shows the confidence score as a percentage
    And each card has "Approve" and "Dismiss" buttons

  Scenario: Approve a pending learning without edits
    Given Marcus is on the Pending tab
    When Marcus clicks "Approve" on "Avoid module-level mutable singletons for caching"
    And the approve dialog opens
    And Marcus clicks "Approve as Active" without editing
    Then the learning status changes to "active"
    And a success toast shows "Learning approved and activated"
    And the card is removed from the pending list
    And the Pending tab count updates to "(2)"
    And the Active tab count increments

  Scenario: Approve with text edit
    Given Marcus clicks "Approve" on "Log all errors with structured context"
    And the approve dialog opens with editable text
    When Marcus edits the text to "Log all errors with structured context including timestamp and request ID"
    And Marcus clicks "Approve as Active"
    Then the learning is saved with the edited text
    And the learning status changes to "active"
    And the active learnings list shows the updated text

  Scenario: Collision warning during approval
    Given there is an active learning "Do NOT use module-level mutable singletons for caching or shared state"
    When Marcus clicks "Approve" on "Avoid module-level mutable singletons for caching"
    Then the approve dialog shows a collision warning
    And the warning displays the similar active learning text
    And the warning shows similarity: 92%
    And Marcus can still choose to approve or cancel

  Scenario: Dismiss a pending learning with reason
    Given Marcus is on the Pending tab
    When Marcus clicks "Dismiss" on "Use RecordId objects instead of string IDs"
    And the dismiss dialog opens
    And Marcus enters reason "Too specific to current codebase, not a general rule"
    And Marcus clicks "Dismiss"
    Then the learning status changes to "dismissed"
    And a success toast shows "Learning dismissed"
    And the card is removed from the pending list
    And the reason is stored with the learning record

  Scenario: Dismiss requires a reason
    Given Marcus clicks "Dismiss" on a pending learning
    When the dismiss dialog opens
    Then the reason field is required
    And the "Dismiss" button is disabled until a reason is entered

  Scenario: Cancel approval returns to pending list
    Given Marcus opens the approve dialog for a pending learning
    When Marcus clicks "Cancel"
    Then the dialog closes
    And the learning remains in pending status
    And no changes are made

  Scenario: Undo approval via toast
    Given Marcus just approved a learning
    And the success toast is visible with an "Undo" action
    When Marcus clicks "Undo" within the toast timeout
    Then the learning reverts to "pending_approval" status
    And the pending count re-increments

# Definition of Ready Validation: Learning Library

## US-LL-01: Browse & Filter Learning Library

| DoR Item | Status | Evidence |
|----------|--------|----------|
| Problem statement clear, domain language | PASS | "He cannot answer the basic question: What rules are my agents following right now?" -- clear pain in domain terms |
| User/persona identified | PASS | "Workspace owner managing four AI agent types" with specific agent list |
| 3+ domain examples with real data | PASS | 3 examples: Marcus filtering by mcp (12 -> 5 learnings), no-results filter, Priya's empty workspace |
| UAT scenarios (3-7) | PASS | 6 scenarios: navigate, browse, filter, expand, empty state, tab switch |
| AC derived from UAT | PASS | 9 AC items derived from scenarios |
| Right-sized (1-3 days, 3-7 scenarios) | PASS | ~2-3 days (page route, list component, filter logic, card component, empty states). 6 scenarios. |
| Technical notes | PASS | API endpoints identified, routing pattern, sidebar integration, shared constant gap noted |
| Dependencies tracked | PASS | Existing API (complete), shared contracts (complete), new AGENT_TYPES constant (needed) |

**DoR Status**: PASSED

## US-LL-03: Edit or Deactivate Active Learning

| DoR Item | Status | Evidence |
|----------|--------|----------|
| Problem statement clear, domain language | PASS | "coding agents are being overly rigid about TypeScript strict mode" -- concrete behavioral issue |
| User/persona identified | PASS | Marcus (edit flow), Priya (deactivation flow) -- distinct scenarios |
| 3+ domain examples with real data | PASS | 3 examples: edit text to add nuance, narrow agent targeting, deactivate conflicting learning |

| Dependencies tracked | PASS | US-LL-01 (expanded card), backend gaps flagged as potential blockers |

content.text:
All artifacts updated. Here's what changed:

**US-LL-02 simplified from "Triage Pending" to "Inline Pending Actions":**

- **Before**: Dedicated Pending tab with a separate triage workflow, ~2 days
- **After**: Pending cards show inline Approve/Dismiss buttons in the regular library list, ~1 day

**Key changes:**
- No dedicated Pending tab — pending is just a status filter value in US-LL-01's filter controls
- Pending cards show action buttons inline; active/dismissed/deactivated cards don't
- Approve and dismiss dialogs are shared components extracted from the existing feed implementation
- Feed remains the push notification layer; library is the pull management layer
- Estimate dropped from 2 days to 1 day (reuses feed dialog components)

**Total estimate**: 6-8 days (down from 7-9).

Anything else you'd like to adjust before we're ready for `/nw:design`?

Do NOT explain why there is nothing to log.

Otherwise, check each category:

1. **Decisions**: Architecture or design choices where alternatives existed (e.g. choosing a pattern, library, data model, or trade-off). Do NOT log mechanical bugfixes, typo corrections, or changes with only one correct approach. Call create_provisional_decision for each genuine decision.
2. **Questions**: Unresolved questions raised but not logged. Call ask_question for each.
3. **Observations**: Contradictions, duplication, missing items, deprecated patterns, or anomalies noticed. Call log_observation for each.
4. **Task progress**: Tasks worked on whose status wasn't updated. Call update_task_status for each.
5. **Subtasks identified**: Work items or follow-up tasks discussed but not created. Call create_subtask for each (requires a parent task).
6. **Suggestions**: Optimizations, risks, opportunities, missing elements, or potential pivots noticed during work. Call create_suggestion for each with category (optimization|risk|opportunity|conflict|missing|pivot), rationale, and confidence (0-1).

After logging everything, respond with valid JSON in this exact shape:
{"decision":"approve","summary":"<one-line session summary>","decisions_made":["<decision-id>"],"questions_asked":["<question-id>"],"tasks_progressed":[{"task_id":"<task-id>","from_status":"<from>","to_status":"<to>"}],"files_changed":[{"path":"<path>","change_type":"created|modified|deleted"}],"observations_logged":["<observation-id>"],"subtasks_created":["<subtask-id>"],"suggestions_created":["<suggestion-id>"]}

If you cannot log required items (for example MCP unavailable), respond with:
{"decision":"block","reason":"Log these items first: ..."}

content.text:
# NW-DESIGN: Architecture Design

**Wave**: DESIGN (wave 3 of 6) | **Agents**: Morgan (nw-solution-architect) | **Command**: `*design-architecture`

## Overview

Execute DESIGN wave through discovery-driven architecture design. Morgan asks about business drivers and constraints first, then recommends architecture that fits. Analyzes existing codebase, evaluates open-source alternatives, produces C4 diagrams (Mermaid) as mandatory output.

## Context Files Required

- docs/feature/{feature-name}/discuss/requirements.md
- docs/feature/{feature-name}/discuss/user-stories.md
- docs/feature/{feature-name}/discuss/domain-model.md
- docs/feature/{feature-name}/discuss/ux-journey.md
- docs/feature/{feature-name}/discuss/jtbd-job-stories.md — Job stories (JTBD Phase 1)
- docs/feature/{feature-name}/discuss/jtbd-four-forces.md — Four Forces analysis
- docs/feature/{feature-name}/discuss/jtbd-opportunity-scores.md — Opportunity scores (if multiple jobs)
- docs/feature/{feature-name}/design/constraints.md — Technical and business constraints

## Discovery Flow

Architecture decisions driven by quality attributes, not pattern shopping:

### Step 1: Understand the Problem
Review JTBD artifacts from DISCUSS to understand which jobs the architecture must serve. Morgan asks: What are we building? For whom? Which quality attributes matter most? (scalability|maintainability|testability|time-to-market|fault tolerance|auditability)

### Step 2: Understand Constraints
Morgan asks: Team size/experience? Timeline? Existing systems to integrate? Regulatory requirements? Operational maturity (CI/CD, monitoring)?

### Step 3: Team Structure (Conway's Law)
Morgan asks: How many teams? Communication patterns? Does proposed architecture match org chart?

### Step 3.5: Development Paradigm Selection

Morgan identifies primary language(s) from constraints, then applies:

- **FP-native** (Haskell|F#|Scala|Clojure|Elixir): recommend Functional
- **OOP-native** (Java|C#|Go): recommend OOP
- **Multi-paradigm** (TypeScript|Kotlin|Python|Rust|Swift): present both, let user choose based on team experience and domain fit

After confirmation, ask user permission to write paradigm to project CLAUDE.md:
- FP: `This project follows the **functional programming** paradigm. Use @nw-functional-software-crafter for implementation.`
- OOP: `This project follows the **object-oriented** paradigm. Use @nw-software-crafter for implementation.`

Default if user declines/unsure: OOP. User can override later.

### Step 4: Recommend Architecture Based on Drivers
Recommend based on quality attribute priorities|constraints|paradigm from Steps 1-3.5. Default: modular monolith with dependency inversion (ports-and-adapters). Overrides require evidence.

If functional paradigm selected, Morgan adapts architectural strategy:
- Types-first design: define algebraic data types and domain models before components
- Composition pipelines: data flows through transformation chains, not method dispatch
- Pure core / effect shell: domain logic is pure, IO lives at boundaries (adapters are functions)
- Effect boundaries replace port interfaces: function signatures serve as ports
- Immutable state: state changes produce new values, no mutation in the domain
These are strategic guidance items for the architecture document — no code snippets.

### Step 5: Advanced Architecture Stress Analysis (HIDDEN -- `--residuality` flag only)
When activated: apply complexity-science-based stress analysis — stressors|attractors|residues|incidence matrix|resilience modifications. See `stress-analysis` skill.
When not activated: skip entirely, do not mention.

### Step 6: Produce Deliverables
- Architecture document with component boundaries|tech stack|integration patterns
- C4 System Context diagram (Mermaid) -- MANDATORY
- C4 Container diagram (Mermaid) -- MANDATORY
- C4 Component diagrams (Mermaid) -- only for complex subsystems
- ADRs for significant decisions

## Rigor Profile Integration

Before dispatching the architect agent, read rigor config from `.nwave/des-config.json` (key: `rigor`). If absent, use standard defaults.

- **`agent_model`**: Pass as `model` parameter to Task tool. If `"inherit"`, omit `model` (inherits from session).
- **`reviewer_model`**: If design review is performed, use this model for the reviewer agent. If `"skip"`, skip design review.
- **`review_enabled`**: If `false`, skip post-design review step.

## Agent Invocation

@nw-solution-architect

Execute \*design-architecture for {feature-name}.

Context files: see Context Files Required above.

**Configuration:**
- model: rigor.agent_model (omit if "inherit")
- interactive: moderate
- output_format: markdown
- diagram_format: mermaid (C4)
- stress_analysis: {true if --residuality flag, false otherwise}

## Success Criteria

- [ ] Business drivers and constraints gathered before architecture selection
- [ ] Existing system analyzed before design (codebase search performed)
- [ ] Integration points with existing components documented
- [ ] Reuse vs. new component decisions justified
- [ ] Architecture supports all business requirements
- [ ] Technology stack selected with clear rationale
- [ ] Development paradigm selected and (optionally) written to project CLAUDE.md
- [ ] Component boundaries defined with dependency-inversion compliance
- [ ] C4 System Context + Container diagrams produced (Mermaid)
- [ ] ADRs written with alternatives considered
- [ ] Handoff accepted by nw-platform-architect (DEVOP wave)

## Next Wave

**Handoff To**: nw-platform-architect (DEVOP wave)
**Deliverables**: See Morgan's handoff package specification in agent file

## Expected Outputs

```
docs/feature/{feature-name}/design/
  architecture-design.md       (includes C4 diagrams in Mermaid)
  technology-stack.md
  component-boundaries.md
  data-models.md
docs/adrs/
  ADR-NNN-*.md
CLAUDE.md (project root)   (optional: ## Development Paradigm section)
```


ARGUMENTS: learning-library
